# workbench_folder (Resource)

Workbench provisioned folder resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "workbench_folder" "tf_folder" {
  workspace_id = workbench_workspace.example_workspace.id
  display_name = "terraform-example-folder"
  description  = "test folder"
}

output "my_folder" {
  value = workbench_folder.tf_folder
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->

## Schema

### Required

- `display_name` (String) Workbench Folder display name
- `workspace_id` (String) Folder ID to which the folder belongs

### Optional

- `description` (String) Workbench Folder description
- `parent_folder_id` (String) Parent folder ID of the Folder, if any
- `properties` (Attributes Set) Workbench Folder properties in key-value pair (see
  [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--properties))

### Read-Only

- `created_by` (String) Workbench Folder created by
- `created_date` (String) Workbench Folder created date
- `id` (String) Workbench folder unique identifier
- `last_updated_by` (String) Workbench Folder last updated by
- `last_updated_date` (String) Workbench Folder last updated date

<a id="nestedatt--properties"></a>

### Nested Schema for `properties`

Required:

- `key` (String) Key of the property
- `value` (String) Value of the property

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the
[`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the
`id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = workbench_folder.my_folder
  id = "workspaces/{{workspace_uuid}}/resources/{{folder_id}}"
}
```
